subject: literacy
title: Grade 1 Literacy
description: Letter sounds, CVC words, sight words, and simple sentences.

lessons.title: Letter Sounds (Phonics)
lessons.objective: Learn the sounds that letters make and how to blend them.
lessons.content:
Every letter makes a SOUND. When we know the sounds, we can READ words!

🔤 Some letter sounds:
  s — sounds like a snake: ssss
  a — sounds like you're opening wide at the dentist: aaa
  t — sounds like tapping: t-t-t
  p — sounds like popping: p-p-p
  n — sounds like humming: nnn

📖 BLENDING means pushing sounds together to make words:
  s + a + t = sat
  p + a + n = pan
  t + a + p = tap

Practice: Point to each letter, say its sound, then blend them together!
  C-A-T → Cat! 🐱
  D-O-G → Dog! 🐶

exercises.question: If you blend the sounds s-u-n together, what word do you get?
exercises.hint: Say each sound slowly: sss — uuu — nnn. Now say them faster!
exercises.explanation: When you blend s + u + n together, you get the word 'sun' — that big bright thing in the sky! ☀️

exercises.question: What word do these sounds make: c-a-t? (one word)
exercises.hint: It's a furry pet that says meow!
exercises.explanation: c + a + t = cat! A cat is a small furry animal that purrs and meows. 🐱

lessons.title: Simple Sentences
lessons.objective: Learn what makes a simple sentence.
lessons.content:
A SENTENCE is a group of words that tells us something complete.

Every sentence needs:
  ✅ A CAPITAL LETTER at the start
  ✅ A FULL STOP (.) at the end
  ✅ To make sense!

Examples of good sentences:
  The cat sat on the mat.
  I like to play.
  She can run fast.

❌ Not a sentence: 'the cat' (not complete!)
❌ Not a sentence: 'sat on mat' (missing parts!)

Fingers spaces go between each word so we can read them easily!

exercises.question: What goes at the END of a sentence?
exercises.explanation: Every sentence ends with a full stop (.). It tells us the sentence is finished!

exercises.question: Which of these is a complete sentence?
exercises.hint: A complete sentence starts with a capital letter, makes sense, and ends with a full stop.
exercises.explanation: 'The dog ran fast.' is correct! It has a capital letter, makes complete sense, and ends with a full stop.
